solute.description: This molecule features a bicyclo[2.2.1]heptane (norbornane) skeleton, which is a rigid, bridged bicyclic system consisting of a six-membered ring with a one-carbon bridge. It contains a ketone functional group at the C2 position and is substituted with three methyl groups: one at the C1 bridgehead and two geminal methyls on the C7 bridge. The structure is relatively small (C10H16O) and predominantly nonpolar due to the hydrocarbon framework, though the carbonyl group introduces a localized dipole and serves as a hydrogen-bond acceptor. Its compact, globular 3D conformation and the presence of the strained bridged system make it a sterically hindered and chemically stable molecule.

solvent.description: This molecule is a para-substituted aromatic hydrocarbon consisting of a central benzene ring with a methyl group and an isopropyl group located at the 1 and 4 positions, respectively. It is an alkylbenzene characterized by its purely hydrocarbon framework, featuring a branched aliphatic side chain and a simple methyl substituent. Due to the absence of heteroatoms, the structure is entirely non-polar and lacks any hydrogen bond donors or acceptors. With a molecular formula of C₁₀H₁₄, it is a relatively small, hydrophobic molecule defined by its symmetrical para-substitution pattern.
